input.background: In Exercise 3.2, you created a stemplot of lengths of hospital stays for 25 patients. Table 3.6 lists the data.
input.question: Construct a frequency table for these data using 5-day class intervals. Include columns for the frequency counts, relative frequencies, and cumulative frequencies.

output.answer:
Frequency table

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}\hline Duration (days) & Frequency & Relative Frequency (\%) & Cumulative Frequency (\%) \\ \hline 0-4 & 5 & 20.0\% & 20\% \\ \hline 5-9 & 12 & 48.0 & 68 \\ \hline 10-14 & 6 & 24.0 & 92 \\ \hline 15-19 & 1 & 4.0 & 96 \\ \hline 20-24 & 0 & 0.0 & 96 \\ \hline 25-29 & 0 & 0.0 & 96 \\ \hline 30-34 & 1 & 4.0 & 100\% \\ \hline Total & 25 & 100\% & - \\ \hline \end{tabular}

hospital_stay_lengths.description: Lengths of hospital stays for 25 patients in days.

frequency_counts.value: To be calculated based on 5-day class intervals
frequency_counts.description: Frequency counts of hospital stay lengths within 5-day class intervals.

relative_frequencies.value: To be calculated as the proportion of each frequency count to the total number of observations
relative_frequencies.description: Relative frequencies of hospital stay lengths within 5-day class intervals.

cumulative_frequencies.value: To be calculated as the cumulative sum of frequency counts across class intervals
cumulative_frequencies.description: Cumulative frequencies of hospital stay lengths within 5-day class intervals.
